comment: Inventory of baked-in tools in the agent-code-server Docker image. Every tool must have a monitor type.

tools.name: code-server
tools.sourceDetail: ARG CODE_SERVER_VERSION=4.99.3, curl GitHub release .deb — not a Docker FROM reference, so Dependabot docker cannot bump the ARG

tools.name: node
tools.sourceDetail: curl + tar extract in Dockerfile (direct URL, not a Docker FROM reference — Dependabot docker cannot bump this)

tools.name: npm
tools.sourceDetail: Bundled with Node.js tarball from nodejs.org
tools.intentionalRationale: npm version is determined by the Node.js release (shipped together in the tarball). Pinning Node pins npm implicitly.

tools.name: bun
tools.sourceDetail: FROM oven/bun:1.3.14@sha256:... AS bun-runtime — Dependabot docker monitors FROM image tags

tools.name: fixuid
tools.sourceDetail: curl GitHub release tarball in Dockerfile (direct URL, not a Docker FROM reference — Dependabot docker cannot bump this)

tools.name: docker
tools.sourceDetail: COPY --from=docker-dind (FROM docker:29.5.2-dind stage) — Dependabot docker monitors FROM image tags

tools.name: dockerd
tools.sourceDetail: COPY --from=docker-dind (FROM docker:29.5.2-dind stage) — same image as docker CLI

tools.name: gosu
tools.sourceDetail: apt-get install gosu (debian:13-slim package, not version-pinned)
tools.intentionalRationale: gosu comes from Debian stable apt repository. It is a simple su-exec-like tool with no significant breaking changes. Pinning would require manual updates for no benefit. Debian security team handles CVE patches.

tools.name: dumb-init
tools.sourceDetail: apt-get install dumb-init (debian:13-slim package, not version-pinned)
tools.intentionalRationale: dumb-init comes from Debian stable apt repository. It is a minimal init wrapper with no significant version churn. Debian security team handles CVE patches.

tools.name: git
tools.sourceDetail: apt-get install git git-lfs (debian:13-slim package, not version-pinned)
tools.intentionalRationale: Git comes from Debian stable apt repository. Debian security team backports critical patches to the stable release. Pinning a specific git version would not meaningfully improve security or reliability.

tools.name: curl
tools.sourceDetail: apt-get install curl (debian:13-slim package, not version-pinned)
tools.intentionalRationale: curl comes from Debian stable apt repository. Debian security team backports critical patches. Frequently used as a foundational tool in Dockerfiles, not a risk vector on its own.

tools.name: jq
tools.sourceDetail: apt-get install jq (debian:13-slim package, not version-pinned)
tools.intentionalRationale: jq comes from Debian stable apt repository. No breaking changes in minor versions; Debian security team backports patches.

tools.name: python3
tools.sourceDetail: apt-get install python3 python3-pip python3-venv (debian:13-slim package, not version-pinned)
tools.intentionalRationale: Python3 comes from Debian stable apt repository. Debian security team backports patches. Version is tied to Debian release cycle; pinning would require custom backporting with no security benefit.

tools.name: pip
tools.sourceDetail: apt-get install python3-pip (debian:13-slim package, not version-pinned; symlinked to /usr/local/bin/pip)
tools.intentionalRationale: pip comes from Debian stable apt repository via python3-pip. Same rationale as python3: tied to Debian release, security patches backported by the Debian security team.

tools.name: tmux
tools.sourceDetail: apt-get install tmux (debian:13-slim package, not version-pinned)
tools.intentionalRationale: tmux comes from Debian stable apt repository. It is user-facing but low-churn, and Debian security/stability updates are preferred over manual pin maintenance.

tools.name: paseo-relay
tools.sourceDetail: ARG PASEO_RELAY_VERSION=0.5.0, wget GitHub release tarball in paseo-relay-builder stage — not a Docker FROM reference, so Dependabot docker cannot bump the ARG

tools.name: paseo
tools.sourceDetail: RUN npm install -g --prefix /opt/paseo @getpaseo/cli@0.1.105 @getpaseo/server@0.1.105 in Dockerfile.dockerfile — not a Docker FROM reference, so Dependabot docker cannot bump it
